articles.title: Starter homes becoming ‘thing of the past’ in Greater Boston as buyers enter market at age 40
articles.description:
First-time homebuyers are searching for homes they can grow into rather than trade up quickly

The post Starter homes becoming ‘thing of the past’ in Greater Boston as buyers enter market at age 40 appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: First-time homebuyers in Greater Boston are now entering the market at an average age of 40.
Buyers are seeking larger homes to accommodate future growth instead of starter homes.
This trend may reduce housing availability for younger families in the Boston area.
